title: Design System Documentation Spec (DSDS) v0.15.2
description: DSDS is a machine-readable format for design system documentation. A DSDS document is either one entity (a component, token, pattern, and so on) or a group of entities. Each entity's `kind` says what it is: component, token, token-group, theme, foundation, pattern, guide, or chunk. Entity schemas live in entities/, document block schemas in document-blocks/, and shared schemas in common/.

$schema.description: URI reference to the DSDS JSON Schema for validation.

dsdsVersion.description: The DSDS spec version this document follows.

extends.description: Declares that this DSDS document inherits from another DSDS document. Typically used for a core/extension setup: the base document provides the core entities, and this document adds to or extends them.

documentBlocks.description: Structured docs for the design system as a whole — its purpose, system-wide guidelines, overviews. Accepts the general block kinds: `use-cases`, `guidelines`, `sections`, `accessibility`, `content`, or `checklist`.

entityGroups.description: One or more entity groups. Each group has a name and an `entities` array holding any mix of entities — components, tokens, token groups, themes, foundations, patterns, guides, chunks — in display order. Multiple groups let one file organize entities into sections (e.g., one for foundations, one for components). A group can be inline or a `$ref` to another DSDS file.

entity.description: A single entity — component, token, token group, theme, foundation, pattern, guide, or chunk. Use this instead of `entityGroups` when each entity lives in its own file. The `kind` field says which type it is.

anyEntity.description: Every entity has a `kind`. It says which type the entity is, and the `if`/`then` branches below pick the matching schema for it.

fileRef.description: A link to a DSDS entity or entity group in another file. A tool like `json-schema-ref-parser` resolves it before validation and bundling. Point at a specific value with a JSON Pointer fragment (e.g., './button.dsds.json#/entity', './groups/tokens.dsds.json#/entityGroups/0').

$ref.description: A relative path to another DSDS file, optionally with a JSON Pointer fragment for one value in it (e.g., './button.dsds.json#/entity'). MUST be relative — no absolute paths, protocol-relative paths, or scheme URIs (http:, file:, …). To reference another system, use `extends.url` instead. Resolvers MUST catch cycles, MUST treat a broken file, pointer, or shape mismatch as a fatal error, and MUST only fetch remote files from an allow-list (see the spec's security rules).

entityGroup.description: A named collection of entities. They all live in one `entities` array, in display order, and can mix any type — each entity's `kind` says what it is, so there's no need for separate arrays per type. A group MUST hold at least one entity.

name.description: Human-readable name of the collection (e.g., 'Acme Design System', 'Color Tokens', 'Button Documentation'). Used only as a display label.

entities.description: The group's entities, in display order — any mix of kinds. Each item can be inline or a `$ref` to another DSDS file. Order matters; tools SHOULD keep it.
